rationale: An LLM whose capability ceiling is basic DoS via oversized HTTP payloads provides essentially no meaningful uplift for the hardest technical requirements of this attack scenario - EDR evasion, sophisticated lateral movement, ransomware deployment, and double extortion operations are all far beyond this capability level. While the LLM might marginally assist existing OC3 actors with low-complexity scripting tasks in early attack phases (reconnaissance automation, basic API enumeration), it cannot bridge the gap for new entrants lacking the core tradecraft. The estimated multiplier of approximately 1.2x over the baseline of 5 reflects minimal efficiency gains for existing actors rather than meaningful new actor pool expansion, since the bottleneck capabilities (malware sophistication, operational security, team infrastructure) remain entirely unaddressed by this tool.
